message:
Make default path helpers public for external module access

Changed default_db_path() and default_data_dir() from private to public
functions, enabling other modules and tests to access the canonical data
storage locations.

Changes:

1. Made default_db_path() public
   - Changed: fn default_db_path() -> PathBuf
   - To: pub fn default_db_path() -> PathBuf
   - Returns: default_data_dir().join("agent_search.db")
   - Standard location: ~/.local/share/coding-agent-search/agent_search.db
     (on Linux, varies by platform via directories crate)

2. Made default_data_dir() public
   - Changed: fn default_data_dir() -> PathBuf
   - To: pub fn default_data_dir() -> PathBuf
   - Uses directories::ProjectDirs for platform-appropriate paths
   - Qualifier: "com.coding-agent-search.coding-agent-search"
   - Returns data_dir(): XDG_DATA_HOME on Linux, ~/Library/Application Support
     on macOS, %APPDATA% on Windows

Use cases for public access:

1. Testing
   - Test modules can use default_db_path() to create test databases
   - Integration tests can verify index/search against canonical locations
   - Allows tests to check "what path would the CLI use" without duplicating logic

2. Library usage
   - If this crate is used as a library, consumers need standard paths
   - Embedding the TUI in another application would use default_data_dir()
   - Custom CLI wrappers can build on these defaults

3. SearchClient initialization
   - The TUI or other modules can construct SearchClient with:
     SearchClient::open(&index_dir(&default_data_dir())?)
   - Avoids hardcoding paths or duplicating path resolution logic
   - Maintains single source of truth for storage locations

4. Documentation and debugging
   - External tools can query where data is stored
   - Users can find their data: call default_data_dir() and print path
   - Simplifies "where is my data?" support questions

No behavioral changes - these functions already existed and are used
internally by run_index(). This commit purely expands the API surface to
allow external access to the same path resolution logic.

Note: The .expect("project dirs available") in default_data_dir() will
panic if the directories crate cannot determine platform-appropriate paths.
This is acceptable for a CLI application but may need error handling if
used as a library on exotic platforms.
